consistency.level_and_school: Consistent with standard D&D 5e spell data structure.
consistency.casting_time_and_range: Standard format for casting time ('1 minute') and range ('Self').
consistency.components: Components (V, S, M) are standard, and the material component description is detailed.
consistency.duration: Duration ('1 minute') is consistent with the active questioning period.
consistency.class_restriction: Cleric-only restriction is standard for divine spells of this nature.

quality.completeness: The entry appears complete, containing all necessary fields (slug, name, level, school, ritual, casting time, range, components, duration, description, classes).
quality.description_clarity: The description clearly outlines the function (three yes/no questions), limitations (divine knowledge limits, potential for 'unclear' answers), and mechanical consequences of repeated casting (cumulative 25% chance of no answer per extra casting).
quality.flavor_and_mechanics_integration: The mechanics directly support the flavor of contacting a deity for limited, guarded knowledge.

mechanics.core_function: Provides guaranteed, direct (though potentially limited) answers to three yes/no questions, functioning as a high-certainty information gathering tool.
mechanics.risk_assessment: The built-in risk mechanic (cumulative 25% failure chance per repeat casting before a long rest) effectively balances its powerful information retrieval capability, discouraging spamming the spell.
mechanics.resource_cost: Low casting time and relatively simple material components mean the cost is primarily the spell slot and the risk of failure upon overuse.
mechanics.utility: High utility for campaign plot progression or critical decision-making, provided the deity possesses the necessary knowledge.

analysis.suggested_actions: No corrections needed. The data is clean and mechanically sound.
